abort.reconfigure_failed: Could not load the existing configuration

error.invalid_code: Invalid verification code. Please try again.
error.no_2fa_methods: Two-factor authentication required but no methods available
error.rate_limit: Rate limit exceeded. Please try again later.
error.user_not_found: No account found with this email
error.passkey_only: This account uses passkey login which is not supported in Home Assistant
error.no_meters: No meters found on your account. Please add meters via the Målerportal consumer app first.

user.title: Log in to Målerportal
user.description: Enter your email address to continue

2fa_choice.title: Two-factor authentication
2fa_choice.description: Choose how to verify your identity

2fa_totp.title: Authenticator app
2fa_totp.description: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app

2fa_email.title: Email verification
2fa_email.description: A 6-digit code has been sent to {email}. Enter it below.

entity_selection.title: Select meters
entity_selection.description: Select which meters to add to Home Assistant

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure Målerportal
reconfigure.description: Logged in as {email}. Adjust which meters are active and the displayed currency. Installations marked "no longer in account" can be unchecked to remove them.

abort.fetch_more_history_done: Done! Historical data now covers the last {days} days. It may take a few minutes for the Energy Dashboard to update.
abort.refetch_history_done: Re-fetch triggered on {count} sensor(s). Up to 1 year of history will be re-imported in the background — check the logs and Energy Dashboard in a few minutes.
abort.migrate_meter_history_done: Migration applied. Offset {offset} added to all imports for the target so its chart continues seamlessly from the source. Re-import is running in the background.
abort.not_enough_candidates: Need at least two meter entities to migrate between. Add or restore the relevant installations first.
abort.no_history_sensors: No history sensors found. Please reload the integration first.
abort.debug_logging_enabled: Debug logging enabled at level {level}. Check Home Assistant logs for detailed output.
abort.debug_logging_disabled: Debug logging disabled. Log level reset to WARNING.

error.same_entity: Source and target must be different entities.
error.missing_selection: Please select both a source and a target entity.
error.migration_failed: Could not compute or apply the offset. The source needs at least one stored statistic and the target needs at least one as well — check the logs for details.

init.title: Målerportal options
init.description: Currently covering {days} days of history. What would you like to do?

debug_logging.title: Debug logging
debug_logging.description: Enable or disable detailed debug logging for the Målerportal integration. Logs can be viewed in Settings → System → Logs.

data_description.enable_debug: Turn on detailed logging for troubleshooting
data_description.log_level: DEBUG = most detailed, INFO = moderate, WARNING = only warnings

migrate_meter_history.title: Migrate previous meter
migrate_meter_history.description: When the upstream installation ID changed during a meter swap, the new entity starts its history from scratch instead of continuing the old chart. Pick the source (the orphaned/old entity that holds the previous history) and the target (the current active entity). The integration will compute an offset so the target's chart continues seamlessly from where the source left off.

data_description.source_entity: The entity that holds the historical data from the previous meter — usually shows as 'unavailable' on its device card.
data_description.target_entity: The entity that started fresh after the swap — usually shows the current low meter reading.

data.stale_data_fallback_hours: Stale-data fallback (hours, used until cadence is learned)

data_description.polling_interval: How often to fetch new meter readings (15-120 minutes)
data_description.acoustic_noise_threshold: Acoustic noise level above which a leak is suspected. Default 30 Hz; raise it if you get false alarms.
data_description.acoustic_noise_sustained_hours: How long the noise must stay above the threshold before the leak alarm turns on. Filters out brief spikes from normal use.
data_description.leak_notify_enabled: If on, the integration sends a notification when the leak alarm trips (off → on).
data_description.leak_notify_service: Service to call as 'domain.service'. Default 'persistent_notification.create' shows a card in HA. Use 'notify.mobile_app_<your_device>' for push to your phone.
data_description.stale_data_factor: How many times longer than the meter's observed reporting interval before flagging it as stale. 3.0 = alarm at ~3× the median delay between readings.
data_description.stale_data_fallback_hours: Threshold used during the first few hours after install, before the integration has learned the meter's reporting cadence.

missing_installation.title: Installation no longer in your Målerportal account
missing_installation.description: Installation `{installation_id}` was previously configured but is no longer present in your Målerportal account. This can happen if the installation was removed or if the meter was replaced with a new installation ID upstream. Entities for this installation will go unavailable. To remove it from Home Assistant, reconfigure the integration via the integration's options menu.

stale_data.title: Meter has not reported recently
stale_data.description: {installation} has not delivered new readings for {hours_since} hours. Based on its observed reporting cadence (~{expected_hours}h between readings) the threshold is {threshold_hours}h. Possible causes: communication outage at the utility, mounted meter battery low, or the meter was disconnected. Check upstream and adjust the threshold under Settings → Settings if you want to be alerted earlier or later.

refresh.name: Refresh meter readings
refresh.description: Manually refresh meter readings from Meterportal API

installation_id.name: Installation ID
installation_id.description: Optional. The installation ID to refresh. If not provided, all installations will be refreshed.

fetch_more_history.name: Fetch more history
fetch_more_history.description: Fetch 30 more days of historical meter data for the Energy Dashboard. Each call goes 30 days further back.
